commands.repair_reason: The protected maintainer label, missing real behavior proof, and intentional delivery tradeoff need human maintainer judgment rather than an automated repair PR.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain, credential, or permission regression is visible; the patch is limited to Mattermost routing/authz logic and focused tests. (sha=e7bc597a08451aef1930d6f48fc41799a63717c4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Mattermost post, interaction, and command routing so unresolved channel types are denied or dropped instead of treated as regular channel traffic.

Reproducibility: Partly: source inspection shows current main can route posts and interactions through a missing channel-type fallback, and reactions already use the fail-closed pattern. I did not establish a live failing Mattermost reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label, missing real behavior proof, and intentional delivery tradeoff need human maintainer judgment rather than an automated repair PR.

target.title: fix(mattermost): fail closed on missing channel type [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Mattermost inbound post and interaction routing treated an unavailable channel type as a regular channel.
- Solution: Drop posts and interaction dispatch when channel type cannot be resolved, and default the shared mapper to the most restrictive chat kind for missing values.
- What changed: Added guards in post handling, interaction session routing, interaction dispatch, and command authorization; updated focused regression tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No config, manifest, docs, network, or credential behavior changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Motivation

Mattermost handlers need to know whether an inbound event belongs to a direct, group, or channel conversation before applying the correct routing and authorization behavior. When that lookup is unavailable, processing should stop instead of continuing under a less restrictive classification.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost events with unresolved channel type are not routed as regular channel traffic.
- Real environment tested: Not performed; local focused regression coverage only.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-gating.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.channel-kind.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.authz.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `Test Files 4 passed (4); Tests 21 passed (21)`
- Observed result after fix: Missing channel type maps to `direct` in the shared helper, command authorization denies unavailable channel type, and inbound post handling drops the event before dispatch/session recording.
- What was not tested: Live Mattermost server interaction callback flow.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): N/A

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Missing channel type fell back to `channel` in Mattermost chat-kind resolution.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Message and interaction dispatch paths did not require a resolved channel type before routing.
- Contributing context (if known): Reaction handling already had an explicit missing-type guard, but the same pattern was not applied to the other Mattermost inbound paths.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor-gating.test.ts`, `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.channel-kind.test.ts`, `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.authz.test.ts`, `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Missing Mattermost channel type does not become regular channel traffic and does not dispatch inbound posts.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the shared mapper, command authorization, and inbound monitor behavior without requiring a live Mattermost deployment.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Mattermost events are ignored when OpenClaw cannot resolve the channel type for the event.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Mattermost event] -> [channel type unavailable] -> [classified as channel] -> [continues]

After:
[Mattermost event] -> [channel type unavailable] -> [drop before routing]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux worktree environment
- Runtime/container: Node test wrapper
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Mattermost
- Relevant config (redacted): Mattermost channel config using restrictive DM policy and open group policy in regression coverage

### Steps

1. Run the focused Mattermost regression tests listed above.
2. Confirm missing channel type helper behavior.
3. Confirm unavailable channel type does not dispatch an inbound post.

### Expected

- Focused tests pass.
- Missing channel type fails closed.
- Inbound post handling stops before dispatch/session recording.

### Actual

- Focused tests passed: `Test Files 4 passed (4); Tests 21 passed (21)`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Shared channel type mapping, command authorization with unavailable type, inbound post drop path.
- Edge cases checked: `undefined`, `null`, and empty string channel type.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Mattermost callback behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Mattermost events may be dropped during transient channel lookup failures.
  - Mitigation: This is intentional fail-closed behavior; existing cache retry behavior can recover after the lookup failure window.

AI-assisted: yes
